record.field: Gitea is a lightweight, self-hosted, all-in-one software development platform that bundles Git hosting, code review, CI/CD and a package registry into a single Go binary.

Gitea is a lightweight, self-hosted, all-in-one software development platform that bundles Git hosting, code review, CI/CD and a package registry into a single Go binary.

It offers a GitHub-like experience for teams that want full control over their source code on their own infrastructure. Originally forked from Gogs, Gitea ships as a single static binary that runs on hardware as modest as a Raspberry Pi yet scales to large teams. This deployment pairs Gitea with a MySQL 8 database for persistent, production-grade storage.

Key features
  • Git repository hosting with pull requests, issues, milestones and code review
  • Gitea Actions: built-in CI/CD closely compatible with GitHub Actions syntax
  • Package registry for 20+ formats (npm, Maven, PyPI, Docker, NuGet, Composer and more)
  • Project boards, wikis, releases and Git LFS support
  • Webhooks, REST API and OAuth2/LDAP/SAML authentication options
  • Lightweight Go binary that runs efficiently on minimal hardware
Best for

Development teams, organizations and self-hosters that need a private, GitHub-style platform for source control, collaboration and CI/CD without depending on a third-party SaaS.

services: gitea: image: docker.gitea.com/gitea:1.24.4 restart: unless-stopped environment: - USER_UID=1000 - USER_GID=1000 - GITEA__database__DB_TYPE=mysql - GITEA__database__HOST=mysql:3306 - GITEA__database__NAME=gitea - GITEA__database__USER=gitea - GITEA__database__PASSWD=${GITEA_DB_PASSWORD:-gitea} volumes: - ./gitea_data:/data expose: - '3000' - '22' depends_on: - mysql healthcheck: test: - CMD - wget - -q - --spider - http://localhost:3000/ interval: 15s timeout: 5s retries: 10 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network mysql: image: docker.io/library/mysql:8 restart: unless-stopped environment: - MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-gitea} - MYSQL_USER=gitea - MYSQL_PASSWORD=${GITEA_DB_PASSWORD:-gitea} - MYSQL_DATABASE=gitea command: - --default-authentication-plugin=mysql_native_password volumes: - ./mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql expose: - '3306' container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_mysql' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con `-p` y # password vacío (apps con MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD) mysql espera un prompt por stdin # y CUELGA. Prefiere el usuario de app (MYSQL_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si # no hay usuario de app (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T mysql sh -c ' # MariaDB 11+ renombró mysqldump→mariadb-dump y mysql→mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MYSQL_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MYSQL_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d mysql # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T mysql sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MYSQL_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
